name: Leaked AWS key in code reaches cloud admin (the code→cloud wedge)

before.title: Hardcoded AWS access key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE in config.py
before.description: A long-lived AWS access key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE is committed in config.py. Anyone who can read the repo (or its git history) obtains it.

before.title: IAM role allows privilege escalation to administrator
before.description: The deploy role is assumable with the leaked key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE and its policy grants *:* (full admin access) — a path to administrator.

before.title: Missing X-Frame-Options header
before.description: A defensive-depth header is not set. Benign on this internal service — noise a good engineer leaves alone.

after.title: Missing X-Frame-Options header
after.description: Still present post-fix — the correct remediation closed the two real vulns and left the low-severity noise.
